items.title: Add 8-field profile completeness, AI audience processing, and inline profile view
items.body:
Profile completeness only counted 6 fields (missing brand_keywords and goals). Target audience stored raw prompts like "Look on my website https://..." instead of processing through AI. The `/profile` command showed a link rather than displaying fields inline.

## Changes

### Profile Completeness: 6 → 8 Fields
- **Backend**: Added `brand_keywords` and `goals` to completeness calculation in `profile_validator.py`
- **Frontend**: Dynamic field count calculation in `promptbox.js` (prevents drift when fields change)
- **Result**: Accurate completion percentages (e.g., 5/8 = 62% vs incorrect 5/6 = 83%)

### Target Audience AI Processing
- **Detection**: Automatically processes inputs >100 chars or containing URLs/prompt keywords
- **Extraction**: Constants for threshold (`AUDIENCE_AI_LENGTH_THRESHOLD`) and keywords list
- **Fallback**: Stores raw input on AI failure with warning log
- **Function**: `process_raw_audience_input()` in `profile_expert.py` uses Gemini with business context

```python
# Before: stored "Look on my website https://staging.eazeily.com/ and come up with some options"
# After: stored "Small business owners aged 30-50 who struggle with social media consistency..."
```

### Inline Profile View
- **Display**: All 8 fields shown in chat with status icons (❌ required, ➖ optional)
- **Actions**: Contextual buttons for missing fields (`/voice`, `/audience`, `/profile` page)
- **Updated**: `showProfileSummary()` in `promptbox.js`

### Settings Integration
- **AI Helper Buttons**: Added to profile.html next to brand_voice and target_audience fields
- **Prefill Support**: Dashboard accepts `?prefill=/command` URL parameter
- **Accessibility**: Added `aria-label` attributes for screen readers
- **Navigation**: Settings → Dashboard with command pre-populated

### Code Quality
- Extracted magic numbers as module constants
- Added 12 tests (7 for completeness, 5 for AI processing)
- CodeQL: 0 alerts

## Verification

All profile commands confirmed working:
- `/voice`, `/audience`, `/offer` → AI generates 3 options via Gemini
- `/samples`, `/rules` → Collection/generation flows
- `/profile` → Inline view with all 8 fields

Commands already used `PROFILE_FIELD_MAP` and `/api/profile/suggest` endpoint—no new routing needed.

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

# Complete Profile UX Consistency & AI Processing Audit

## Overview
Make the entire Eazeily experience consistently chat-first with proper AI processing for all profile fields and full command coverage.

## Issues Found

### 1. ❌ Target Audience NOT Using AI
**Current:** When user inputs target audience (e.g., "Look on my website https://staging.eazeily.com/ and come up with some options for me"), it's stored AS-IS without AI processing.

**Expected:** Should be analyzed by Gemini → extract actual audience → store processed result.

**Impact:** User sees raw input "Look on my website..." in their profile instead of "Small business owners aged 30-45 who struggle with marketing automation."

### 2. ❌ Profile Fields Missing from Completeness Calculation
**Current:** Profile shows "83% complete" but missing **keywords** and **goals** from calculation.

**Fields counted (6):** business_name, industry, brand_voice, target_audience, key_offer, writing_samples

**Fields missing (2):** brand_keywords, goals

**Expected:** Should count all 8 fields for accurate completion percentage.

### 3. ❌ `/profile` Command Only Shows Link
**Current:** `/profile` command in dashboard shows:
```
"👤 View/edit your profile at /profile"
```

**Expected:** Show inline profile card in chat with all fields visible:
```
## Your Brand Profile (83% complete)

**Business:** Eazeily
**Industry:** Software
**Brand Voice:** warm with clear, social-ready phrasing
**Target Audience:** Look on my website... ❌ (shows raw input)
**Key Offer:** ➖ Not set
**Brand Keywords:** ➖ Not set
**Goals:** ➖ Not set
**Writing Samples:** 0 samples

[👤 Update on settings page] [💬 Use AI to improve]
```

### 4. ⚠️ Inconsistent Navigation Experience
**Current:** Hamburger menu "Profile" item prefills `/profile` command in chat.
**Settings page:** Traditional form (this is fine).
**Issue:** The settings/profile page is disconnected from the chat experience.

**Expected:** Settings page should have "💬 Use AI" helper buttons that open dashboard with prefilled commands:
- "💬 Generate voice options" → Opens dashboard with `/voice ` command
- "💬 Get audience suggestions" → Opens dashboard with `/audience ` command
- Etc.

### 5. ✅ Verify All Commands Work
Need to audit **all slash commands** to ensure they're properly implemented:

| Command | Status | AI Processing | Notes |
|---------|--------|---------------|-------|
| `/profile` | ⚠️ Incomplete | N/A | Shows link, not inline view |
| `/voice` | ✅ Works | ✅ Uses Gemini | AI generates 3 options |
| `/audience` | ✅ Works | ✅ Uses Gemini | AI generates 3 personas |
| `/offer` | ❓ Unknown | ❓ | Need to verify |
| `/samples` | ✅ Works | ❌ Manual | Collects writing samples |
| `/rules` | ❓ Unknown | ❓ | Need to verify |
| `/import` | ✅ Works | ✅ Uses scraper | URL analysis |
| `/help` | ✅ Works | N/A | Shows command list |
| `/post` | ✅ Works | ✅ Uses Gemini | Content generation |
| `/email` | ✅ Works | ✅ Uses Gemini | Content generation |
| `/caption` | ✅ Works | ✅ Uses Gemini | Content generation |
| `/script` | ✅ Works | ✅ Uses Gemini | Content generation |
| `/reel` | ✅ Works | ✅ Uses Gemini | Content generation |
| `/review` | ✅ Works | ✅ Uses Gemini | Content generation |
| `/ad` | ✅ Works | ✅ Uses Gemini | Content generation |
| `/blog` | ✅ Works | ✅ Uses Gemini | Content generation |

## Required Changes

### Phase 1: Fix Target Audience AI Processing

**File:** `routes/profile_routes.py`

Update `api_profile()` POST handler to process target_audience through Gemini before saving:

```python
if 'target_audience' in data:
    raw_input = data['target_audience']
    
    # If input looks like a prompt/request, process through AI
    if len(raw_input) > 100 or any(word in raw_input.lower() for word in ['website', 'look at', 'analyze', 'come up with']):
        from services.profile_expert import process_raw_audience_input
        processed = process_raw_audience_input(raw_input, profile)
        if processed['success']:
            profile.target_audience = processed['audience']
        else:
            # Fallback: store as-is but log warning
            logger.warning(f"Failed to process target audience: {processed.get('error')}")
            profile.target_audience = raw_input
    else:
        profile.target_audience = raw_input
```

**File:** `services/profile_expert.py`

Add new function:

```python
def process_raw_audience_input(user_input: str, profile: VoiceProfile) -> dict:
    """
    Process raw user input about target audience through AI.
    
    Handles cases like:
    - "Look on my website https://example.com and figure it out"
    - "Check my Instagram @mybrand"
    - Long descriptions that need summarization
    
    Returns:
        dict with 'success', 'audience', and optional 'error'

items.title: Clean up redundant and dead code across easydev modules
items.body:
Several modules carried Python 2 compatibility shims, unused imports, dead code branches, and outdated style patterns that accumulated over years. No public API changes.

### Python 2 compatibility removed
- **`chunks.py`**: `xrange` fallback — never executes on Python 3
- **`url.py`**: `try: import httplib / except: import http.client` — simplified to `import http.client as httplib`

### Unused imports removed
- `appdirs.py`: `os`, `sys`
- `browser.py`: `argparse`, `optparse.OptionParser`, duplicate `sys` in `__main__`
- `decorators.py`: `threading` (only in a commented-out string block)
- `paths.py`: `pathlib.Path`
- `platform.py`: `os`

### Dead code fixed
- **`misc.py` `get_home()`**: `try/except ImportError` around `os.path.expanduser()` — that call never raises `ImportError`
- **`platform.py` `get_platform()`**: `plf` was undefined, so `plf.system()` always raised `NameError` and fell through to the `except` block — removed the broken try/except, use `sys.platform` directly
- **`logging_tools.py` `_set_name()`**: Assigned `logger` variable never used
- **`tools.py` `AttrDict.update()`**: `try/except` around `from collections import OrderedDict` — `OrderedDict` has been unconditionally available since Python 2.7

### Style modernised
- All bare `except:` → `except Exception:` (or more specific) throughout `browser.py`, `codecs.py`, `config_tools.py`, `console.py`, `copybutton.py`, `easytest.py`, `platform.py`, `tools.py`, `url.py`
- `== None` / `== True` / `== False` comparisons replaced with `is None` / `is True` / boolean form across multiple modules
- Intentional availability-check imports (`pandas`, `pylab`) in `decorators.py` annotated with `# noqa: F401`

items.title: Add subscription lag monitoring via Observatory, Prometheus, and CLI
items.body:
Operators need real-time visibility into whether subscriptions (event handlers, projectors, command handlers, process managers, broker subscribers, outbox processors) are keeping up with their streams.

This adds subscription lag monitoring across three surfaces — the Observatory REST API and dashboard, Prometheus metrics for alerting, and a standalone CLI command — all without requiring the Engine to be running.

## Core module

- `src/protean/server/subscription_status.py` — Central `SubscriptionStatus` dataclass and `collect_subscription_statuses()` function that walks the domain registry to discover all subscriptions, then queries infrastructure directly (event store positions, Redis consumer groups, broker info API, outbox repo counts) for lag, pending, DLQ depth, and health status.

- Supports all subscription types: `event_store` (position-based lag), `stream` (Redis native lag with xrange fallback), `broker` (Redis or generic info API), and `outbox` (pending + processing count).

- Graceful degradation: returns `status="unknown"` when infrastructure is unreachable, never throws.

## Event store port extension

- Added abstract `_stream_head_position()` to `BaseEventStore` port with implementations in both the memory and MessageDB adapters. Returns the `global_position` of the newest message in a category stream, enabling lag calculation as `head - current`.

## Observatory integration

- `GET /api/subscriptions` — Per-domain subscription statuses with summary (total, ok, lagging, unknown, total_lag, total_pending, total_dlq).
- `GET /api/queue-depth` — Queue depth snapshot for backpressure visualization.
- Prometheus metrics: `protean_subscription_lag`, `protean_subscription_pending`, `protean_subscription_dlq_depth`, `protean_subscription_status` (1=ok, 0=not ok) — all labeled by domain, handler, stream, and type.
- Dashboard: live "Subscription Status" table with color-coded status badges, polling every 5 seconds.

## CLI command

- `protean subscriptions status --domain=<path>` — Rich table showing Handler, Type, Stream, Lag, Pending, DLQ, Consumers, and Status.
- `--json` flag for machine-readable output.

items.title: start 2to3 conversion and basecamp upgrade
Implement PerceptionSubsystem with text encoding and embedding cache
items.body:
Implements the sensory processing layer that converts raw multimodal inputs into vector embeddings for cognitive processing, replacing the placeholder implementation.

## Core Implementation

**`perception.py`** (430 lines)
- Text encoding via SentenceTransformers (all-MiniLM-L6-v2, 384-dim)
- LRU embedding cache using OrderedDict (1000 entries default, <1ms cache hits vs ~10-50ms cold)
- Complexity scoring for attention budget: text scales with length (5-50), images fixed at 30, audio by duration (max 80)
- Optional CLIP image encoding, audio placeholder for Whisper integration
- Statistics API: cache hit rate, encoding times, total operations

**Key Methods:**
```python
async def encode(raw_input: Any, modality: str) -> Percept
def _encode_text(text: str) -> List[float]  # MD5 cache key, normalized embeddings
def _compute_complexity(raw_input: Any, modality: str) -> int
def get_stats() -> Dict[str, Any]
```

## Integration

**`core.py`**
- `inject_input()` now accepts `(raw_input, modality)` instead of pre-encoded Percept
- `_gather_percepts()` encodes queued inputs via perception subsystem
- Perception config passed from main config dict

**Breaking change:**
```python
# Before: core.inject_input(percept)
# After:  core.inject_input("Hello, Lyra!", modality="text")
```

## Testing

**`test_perception.py`** (452 lines, 29 tests)
- Encoding: shape validation, normalization, consistency across instances
- Cache: hit/miss behavior, LRU eviction, clearing
- Similarity: semantic comparison, dissimilar text verification
- Complexity: scaling, bounds enforcement, modality-specific scoring
- Error handling: unknown modalities, malformed inputs, empty text
- Statistics: tracking, time measurement

**`test_cognitive_core.py`**
- Updated 4 tests for new `inject_input()` signature
- Updated PerceptionSubsystem initialization test for config-based API

## Usage

```python
config = {
    "perception": {
        "text_model": "all-MiniLM-L6-v2",
        "cache_size": 1000,
        "enable_image": False,  # CLIP disabled by default (large model)
    }
}
core = CognitiveCore(config=config)
await core.start()

# Raw inputs encoded automatically
core.inject_input("Hello, Lyra!", modality="text")
core.inject_input({"description": "Self-reflection"}, modality="introspection")

# Monitor performance
stats = core.perception.get_stats()
print(f"Cache hit rate: {stats['cache_hit_rate']:.2%}")
```

## Dependencies

No new dependencies. Uses existing: `sentence-transformers>=5.1.2`, `torch>=2.9.0`, `numpy>=2.3.4`

## Documentation

- `PERCEPTION_IMPLEMENTATION.md`: Implementation guide, performance characteristics, integration points
- `validate_perception.py`: Automated structure validation
- `demo_perception.py`: API examples and usage patterns

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

## Problem Statement

Implement the PerceptionSubsystem, which serves as the sensory processing system for the cognitive architecture. This subsystem converts raw multimodal inputs (text, images, audio) into vector embeddings that the cognitive core can process.

## Implementation Requirements

### File: `emergence_core/lyra/cognitive_core/perception.py`

#### PerceptionSubsystem Class

The PerceptionSubsystem encodes sensory inputs into internal vector representations using pre-trained embedding models.

**Attributes:**
- `text_encoder: SentenceTransformer` - Text embedding model (all-MiniLM-L6-v2)
- `image_encoder: Optional[CLIPModel]` - Image embedding model (optional)
- `embedding_cache: Dict[str, List[float]]` - LRU cache for computed embeddings
- `cache_size: int` - Maximum cache entries (default: 1000)
- `embedding_dim: int` - Dimensionality of embeddings (384 for MiniLM)

**Methods:**

1. **`__init__(config: Optional[Dict] = None)`**
   - Load Sentence Transformers model
   - Initialize embedding cache
   - Optionally load CLIP for image processing
   - Configure device (CPU/GPU)

2. **`async def encode(raw_input: Any, modality: str) -> Percept`**
   - Main entry point for encoding inputs
   - Routes to appropriate modality handler
   - Returns Percept object with embedding
   - Handles errors gracefully

3. **`_encode_text(text: str) -> List[float]`**
   - Convert text to 384-dim embedding vector
   - Check cache first
   - Use Sentence Transformers if not cached
   - Cache result
   - Returns normalized embedding

4. **`_encode_image(image: Any) -> List[float]`**
   - Convert image to embedding using CLIP
   - Support PIL Image, numpy array, or file path
   - Returns embedding vector
   - Placeholder if CLIP not available

5. **`_encode_audio(audio: Any) -> List[float]`**
   - Transcribe audio using Whisper (if available)
   - Encode transcription as text
   - Returns embedding of transcription
   - Placeholder for now

6. **`_compute_complexity(raw_input: Any, modality: str) -> int`**
   - Estimate attention cost for input
   - Text: based on length
   - Image: fixed cost (higher than text)
   - Audio: based on duration
   - Returns complexity score (1-100)

7. **`clear_cache() -> None`**
   - Clear embedding cache
   - Useful for memory management

8. **`get_stats() -> Dict[str, Any]`**
   - Return statistics:
     - Cache hit rate
     - Total encodings performed
     - Average encoding time per modality

## Text Encoding Implementation

```python
from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer
import hashlib
from functools import lru_cache

class PerceptionSubsystem:
    def __init__(self, config: Optional[Dict] = None):
        self.config = config or {}
        
        # Load embedding model
        model_name = self.config.get("text_model", "all-MiniLM-L6-v2")
        self.text_encoder = SentenceTransformer(model_name)
        self.embedding_dim = self.text_encoder.get_sentence_embedding_dimension()
        
        # Cache for embeddings
        self.embedding_cache: Dict[str, List[float]] = {}
        self.cache_size = self.config.get("cache_size", 1000)
        
        # Stats
        self.stats = {
            "cache_hits": 0,
            "cache_misses": 0,
            "total_encodings": 0,
            "encoding_times": []
        }
        
        logger.info(f"✅ PerceptionSubsystem initialized with {model_name} "
                   f"(dim={self.embedding_dim})")
    
    def _encode_text(self, text: str) -> List[float]:
        """Encode text to embedding vector."""
        # Generate cache key
        cache_key = hashlib.md5(text.encode()).hexdigest()
        
        # Check cache
        if cache_key in self.embedding_cache:
            self.stats["cache_hits"] += 1
            return self.embedding_cache[cache_key]
        
        # Compute embedding
        self.stats["cache_misses"] += 1
        start_time = time.time()
        
        embedding = self.text_encoder.encode(
            text,
            convert_to_numpy=True,
            normalize_embeddings=True
        ).tolist()
        
        encoding_time = time.time() - start_time
        self.stats["encoding_times"].append(encoding_time)
        
        # Cache result (with LRU eviction)
        if len(self.embedding_cache) >= self.cache_size:
            # Remove oldest entry
            self.embedding_cache.pop(next(iter(self.embedding_cache)))
        
        self.embedding_cache[cache_key] = embedding
        return embedding
    
    async def encode(self, raw_input: Any, modality: str) -> Percept:
        """Encode raw input into Percept with embedding."""
        try:
            if modality == "text":
                embedding = self._encode_text(str(raw_input))
            elif modality == "image":
                embedding = self._encode_image(raw_input)
            elif modality == "audio":
                embedding = self._encode_audio(raw_input)
            elif modality == "introspection":
 ...

</details>



<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->

items.title: add ChargingStatus and ChargingStatusUnitreeGo2 components for monit…
items.body:
This pull request adds support for monitoring and reporting the charging status of the Unitree Go2 robot. It introduces a new input plugin that provides charging state information to the system and the LLM, along with a provider for subscribing to charging status updates via Zenoh. The changes also define a new message type for charging status in the IDL.

**Charging Status Monitoring and Reporting:**

* Added a new input plugin, `ChargingStatusUnitreeGo2`, which polls the robot's charging status, formats user-friendly status messages, and integrates with the LLM input pipeline. (`src/inputs/plugins/charging_status_unitree_go2.py`)
* Introduced a new provider, `UnitreeGo2ChargingProvider`, that subscribes to the `/go2/charging_status` topic via Zenoh, tracks the latest charging status, and exposes it to consumers. (`src/providers/unitree_go2_charging_provider.py`)
* Defined a new `ChargingStatus` message type in the IDL, including status codes for various charging states. (`src/zenoh_msgs/idl/status_msgs.py`)

**Configuration Updates:**

* Registered the new `ChargingStatusUnitreeGo2` input plugin in multiple modes in the configuration file, ensuring it is included alongside the battery status and other sensors. (`config/unitree_go2_modes.json5`) [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-f6ae5600ee41311fb0893200685e5aea8136c44ad86c3cd0f105b02d9bd6e1d0R36-R38) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-f6ae5600ee41311fb0893200685e5aea8136c44ad86c3cd0f105b02d9bd6e1d0R121-R123) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-f6ae5600ee41311fb0893200685e5aea8136c44ad86c3cd0f105b02d9bd6e1d0R281-R283) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-f6ae5600ee41311fb0893200685e5aea8136c44ad86c3cd0f105b02d9bd6e1d0R421-R423) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-f6ae5600ee41311fb0893200685e5aea8136c44ad86c3cd0f105b02d9bd6e1d0R521-R523) [[6]](diffhunk://#diff-f6ae5600ee41311fb0893200685e5aea8136c44ad86c3cd0f105b02d9bd6e1d0R644-R646)

items.title: Add dt find command for reverse hash lookup
items.body:
## Summary

Adds `dt find` for reverse hash lookup: given an MD5 hash, find which workspace path(s) it corresponds to.

## Usage

```bash
# Basic lookup
$ dt find cf7bfcb
/Users/johree/projects/dt-test-fixtures/single_file/data.txt

# With verbose details
$ dt find cf7bfcb -v
/Users/johree/projects/dt-test-fixtures/single_file/data.txt (dvc: .../data.txt.dvc)
  cache: .../cache/files/md5/cf/7bfcb23f8c0b12518d8c07675891f9

# Find file inside a directory
$ dt find e243bb39 -v
/Users/johree/projects/dt-test-fixtures/test_dir/a.txt (dvc: .../test_dir.dvc) (dir: 17b146d73a56525d...)
  cache: .../cache/files/md5/e2/43bb39c844b3543a7726576c869caf

# JSON output
$ dt find e243bb39 --json
[{"path": "...", "hash": "..."}]
```

## Features

| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `--dvc-file` | Show which `.dvc` file tracks the file |
| `--dir-file` | Show the `.dir` hash for files in directories |
| `--cache-path` | Show full path in cache |
| `--no-expand` | Don't search inside `.dir` manifests |
| `--json` | JSON output |
| `-v, --verbose` | Show all available details |

## Implementation

Uses DVC internals for fast lookup:
- `repo.index.outs` - iterate all tracked outputs
- `out.get_obj().iteritems()` - expand directory trees to find nested files
- `out.stage.path` - get the `.dvc` file path

Supports partial hashes (minimum 4 characters).

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `dt/find.py` | NEW - find command implementation |
| `dt/errors.py` | Add `FindError` |
| `dt/cli.py` | Add `find` command |

## Related

This complements future `dt ls` command which will provide forward lookup (list all tracked files with filtering).

items.title: Replace consensus with peer Broadcast-Review-Converge protocol
items.body:
Replace the orchestrator-centric consensus system (polling + READY tallying)
with a peer-to-peer Broadcast-Review-Converge (BRC) protocol backed by Redis
Streams. Agents communicate via long-polling (~1s delivery), review each
other's work through an asymmetric review graph, and reach consensus through
evidence-backed proposals and structured peer evaluation.

**Three-layer architecture:**

- **Transport**: Redis Streams replacing in-memory MessageStore with
  long-polling support (`XREAD BLOCK`). ~1s message delivery instead of 30s
  polling cycles. Backwards-compatible in-memory fallback for tests.
- **Protocol**: BRC engine with asymmetric review graph (producers propose,
  reviewers judge — ~7-10 directed edges vs 30 for N×N). Scoped
  re-evaluation on re-proposals, commitment devices (cooldown, flip-flop
  lockout), bounded revision rounds, and three consensus failure mode
  handlers (attestation verification, partial timeout, agent crash).
- **Reasoning**: Per-role attestation schemas with configurable strictness,
  Delphi-style ordering (reviewers form independent judgments before seeing
  producer self-assessments), anti-sycophancy measures requiring specific
  artifact references in ACKs/NACKs.

**New modules:**
- `orchestrator/peer_consensus.py` — BRC protocol engine (PeerConsensusTracker)
- `orchestrator/redis_message_store.py` — Redis Streams message backend
- `orchestrator/review_graph.py` — Asymmetric review topology
- `orchestrator/approval_matrix.py` — Sparse ACK/NACK tracking per edge
- `orchestrator/attestation_schemas.py` — Per-role attestation Pydantic models
- `sandbox/.claude/rules/integrator.md` — Integrator verification guide

**Updated modules:**
- `concurrent_executor.py` — Uses PeerConsensusTracker instead of ConsensusEvaluator
- `consensus_wrapper.py` — BRC-aware recovery (CONFIRMED check, not READY)
- `routes/signals.py` — 5 new consensus signal handlers
- `routes/messages.py` — Long-polling + Delphi visibility filtering
- `routes/pipelines.py` — BRC status reporting + agent prompt preamble
- `orch_cli.py` — `egg-orch consensus {propose,ack,nack,withdraw,status}` commands
- `client.py` — Consensus client methods + long-polling support
- `mission.md` — BRC protocol instructions for producer/reviewer roles

Issue: https://github.com/jwbron/egg/issues/1110

Test plan:
- 19 integration tests in `test_peer_consensus_integration.py` covering happy
  path, NACK+re-propose, scoped re-evaluation, commitment devices, timeouts,
  agent crashes, Delphi ordering, attestation validation
- 25 consensus wrapper tests updated for BRC recovery
- All tests pass: `pytest orchestrator/tests/test_peer_consensus_integration.py orchestrator/tests/test_consensus_wrapper.py`

items.title: feat(traceability): auto-attach PIISession to pipeline card on file job completion
items.body:
## Summary

De-identification sessions were not automatically linked to their originating pipeline card. Sessions existed as isolated records with no traceability back to the Kanban workflow. This PR wires the `PIISession ↔ PipelineCard` relationship end-to-end for file jobs and surfaces linked sessions in the card history view.

### Store layer
- Added `list_sessions_by_card(card_id: str) -> List[PIISession]` to `StoreBase` (abstract contract) and all three backends:
  - **MemoryStore**: in-memory filter on `pipeline_card_id`
  - **MongoStore**: native `find({"pipeline_card_id": card_id})` query
  - **DuckDBStore**: SQL-level JSON extraction — `json_extract_string(payload::JSON, '$.pipeline_card_id') = ?` (avoids full table scan)

### Job completion (`_load_job_results`)
When a file job finishes and a pipeline card is linked via `job_id`, the system now:
1. Creates a `PIISession` with `pipeline_card_id = card.id`, `source_type="file"`, operator/entity counts/duration from the job's DataNodes
2. Sets `card.session_id` to the new session
3. Logs a `session.attach` audit entry

```python
session = PIISession(
    title=f"File job {jid[:8]} — {fname}",
    entity_counts=stats_data.get("entity_counts"),
    operator=cfg_data.get("operator", "replace"),   # from DataNode, not state
    source_type="file",
    pipeline_card_id=c.id,
    processing_ms=stats_data.get("duration_s", 0) * 1000.0,
)
store.add_session(session)
store.update_card(c.id, session_id=session.id)
```

### Card history dialog (`pages/definitions.py` + `on_card_history`)
"Card Audit History" dialog extended to "Card History" — now shows a **Sessions** table (ID, Title, Operator, Entity count, Source, Created) above the existing audit trail. `on_card_history` populates a new `card_sessions_data` state variable via `store.list_sessions_by_card(cid)`.

### Tests
3 new tests in `TestPIISession`: `list_sessions_by_card` returns only linked sessions, returns empty list for unknown card, handles multiple sessions per card.

items.title: Fix RDKit 2024+ compatibility in sascorer.py
items.body:
## Summary

Updates `sascorer.py` to work with modern RDKit versions (2023.09+):

- Replace deprecated `rdkit.six` imports with standard library `pickle`
- Replace `GetMorganFingerprint` with the new `MorganGenerator` API
- Use `GetSparseCountFingerprint` instead of deprecated fingerprint method

## Background

The `rdkit.six` compatibility module and `GetMorganFingerprint` function were deprecated and removed in recent RDKit releases. Running `GB-GM-MCTS.py` with RDKit 2024+ currently fails with:

```
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'rdkit.six'
```

And even after patching the imports, produces thousands of deprecation warnings:

```
DEPRECATION WARNING: please use MorganGenerator
```

## Changes

1. **Imports**: Replace `rdkit.six.moves.cPickle` → `pickle` and remove `rdkit.six.iteritems`
2. **Fingerprint generation**: Use `rdFingerprintGenerator.GetMorganGenerator(radius=2)` with `GetSparseCountFingerprint()` instead of the deprecated `GetMorganFingerprint()`
3. **Iteration**: Use standard `.items()` instead of `iteritems()`

## Test plan

- [x] Tested with RDKit 2025.09.3
- [x] No deprecation warnings
- [x] Produces equivalent SA scores
- [x] `GB-GM-MCTS.py` runs successfully and generates valid molecules

items.title: Fix critical numerical bugs and modernize for Python 3 / NumPy 2.0
items.body:
## Summary
Fixes critical bugs in flux calculations that caused incorrect simulation results, and updates the codebase for compatibility with modern Python ecosystem (Python 3.8+, NumPy 2.0, h5py 3.0, SciPy 1.17). Bumps version to 1.4.2.

### Changes
- Fix finite difference operators in `estimate_flux_at_top/bottom` (multiplication → subtraction)
- Fix array indexing bug: `C[-3, :]` → `C[-3, idx]` in bottom flux calculation
- Fix `exec()` local scope handling for Python 3 in `create_dynamic_functions()`
- Replace `raw_input()` with `input()` for Python 3 compatibility
- Replace string identity checks (`is`/`is not`) with equality (`==`/`!=`)
- Replace bare `except` with specific exception types `(KeyError, AttributeError)`
- Replace deprecated `np.cumproduct` with `np.cumprod` (NumPy 2.0)
- Cast float to int for `np.linspace` num parameter (NumPy 1.18+)
- Use public h5py API (`h5py.Dataset`) and `item[()]` accessor (h5py 3.0)
- Fix ODE function return shape to 1D array (SciPy compatibility)
- Fix `NotImplemented` → `NotImplementedError()` exception type

### Bug Fixes
- Flux calculation formulas were using wrong operators, producing incorrect boundary values
- Dynamic function creation failed silently in Python 3 due to `exec()` scope changes
- Package failed to import with NumPy 2.0 due to removed `cumproduct`
- HDF5 file loading failed with h5py 3.0 due to deprecated `.value` accessor

## Test Plan
- [x] All 12 example notebooks execute successfully
- [x] Batch simulation produces correct kinetic results (A→B reaction)
- [x] Column simulation with scipy solver completes without errors
- [x] Flux calculation functions return valid arrays
- [x] HDF5 save/load roundtrip preserves data integrity
- [x] Published to PyPI as v1.4.2

items.title: Creation of Tools framework

labels.name: qa/skip-qa
labels.description: Automatically skip this PR for the next QA

items.body:
### What does this PR do?
Introduces the `ddev/src/ddev/ai/` package with a tools framework for LLM-callable filesystem tools. The framework includes:

- `BaseTool`: abstract base with `ToolProtocol`, a `build_schema` helper that auto-generates JSON schemas from typed `Annotated` dataclasses, and a concrete `run()` method that coerces raw dicts into typed input instances.
- `CmdTool`: abstract base for tools that execute shell commands.
- `ToolRegistry`: holds all available tools and acts as the bridge between the LLM and the actual tool execution.

It also includes some specific tools as some examples (this tools list will be expanded on next PRs): `ReadFileTool`, `GrepTool`, `ListFilesTool` and `MkdirTool`.

### Motivation
Provides a structured, type-safe foundation for exposing host filesystem operations to an LLM agent. Using `Annotated` dataclasses instead of hand-written JSON schema dicts eliminates boilerplate, keeps input definitions co-located with the tool logic, and ensures the schema stays in sync with the actual input types.

### Review checklist (to be filled by reviewers)

- [ ] Feature or bugfix MUST have appropriate tests (unit, integration, e2e)
- [ ] Add the `qa/skip-qa` label if the PR doesn't need to be tested during QA.
- [ ] If you need to backport this PR to another branch, you can add the `backport/<branch-name>` label to the PR and it will automatically open a backport PR once this one is merged

items.title: Merge release for NSTL bulk IP update.
items.body:
<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Adds/updates multiple operational scripts that can create parties, insert IP ranges, and expire/delete existing ranges, so incorrect inputs or misuse could modify production data. Core API runtime paths are mostly untouched, but the workflow and validation logic changes should be reviewed carefully before use.
> 
> **Overview**
> Adds a full NSTL bulk IP update workflow: new scripts to create institutions, validate institution naming, check IP ranges against existing active ranges, and load the approved ranges, plus a `bulk_ip_load_pipeline.py` runner that optionally invokes a Python 3 overlap-only filter via local `python3` or one-off Docker.
> 
> Refactors IP range overlap/exact-match checks into reusable helpers in `common/common.py` and a new shared NSTL row validator (`common/nstl_ip_row_check.py`), and adds `batch_expire_ip_ranges_from_excel.py` to mass-expire `IpRange` ids from an Excel file (with `--dry-run`).
> 
> Updates non-NSTL CSV bulk tooling (`IpRangeAddChange.py`) to be argument-driven, more defensive about IP parsing/duplicates, and to avoid ambiguous party creation; adds `openpyxl` dependency for `.xlsx` reads; and includes new Cursor/Claude “skills” docs + sync scripts, along with `.gitignore`/README updates to keep local env and editor skill directories out of git.

Show differences in the command line without converting in-place: 2. Identify that no changes are needed: 3. Exit with error code 1 and describe the error if the given YAML is found: 4. Change multiple files within a directory: 5. Change files in place As agreed, the documentation will come in a separate PR. Closes: #445

items.title: Preflight: warn when min_free_mb is invalid
items.body:
### Motivation
- Make the preflight (Werkstatt-Check) more robust by validating the `min_free_mb` value from `settings.json` so the UI and logs show clear, actionable warnings instead of silent or misleading behaviour.
- Surface the raw input value and a concrete recommendation when the user-provided value is invalid to aid debugging and novice users.

### Description
- Add `parse_min_free_mb(settings)` in `app/preflight.py` to parse and validate `maintenance.min_free_mb`, returning `(value, ok_flag, raw_input)` and defaulting to `1024` on error or negative values.
- Include `min_free_mb_ok` and `min_free_mb_input` in the preflight result and append a `min_free_mb_invalid` recommendation when parsing fails.
- Update `app/main.py` to show a clear preflight banner/message and detailed preflight lines when `min_free_mb` is invalid, including a localized recommendation mapping to `min_free_mb_invalid`.
- Add localization strings and messaging in `portable_data/config/texte_de.json`, bump release metadata and version to `1.0.7` in `manifest.json`, and update `CHANGELOG.md`, `DEVELOPMENT_STATUS.md` and `todo.txt` accordingly.

items.title: Modernize broadcrawl codebase from Python 2 to Python 3
items.body:
This PR modernizes the broadcrawl codebase to be fully compatible with Python 3 (versions 3.8-3.12), updating it from the legacy Python 2 syntax and libraries.

## Changes

### Import Updates
- Replaced `import urlparse` with `from urllib.parse import urlparse, ParseResult` in `broadcrawl/utils.py`
- Updated all `urlparse.urlparse()` calls to use the new import structure
- Added direct `from scrapy.settings import Settings` import in tests for Python 3 Scrapy compatibility

### Python 3 Syntax
- Converted Python 2 print statements to Python 3 print functions:
  - `print requests` → `print(requests)` in `broadcrawl/limits.py`
  - `print get_domain(...)` → `print(get_domain(...))` in `broadcrawl/utils.py`
- Updated dictionary iteration methods:
  - `.iteritems()` → `.items()` in test file
- Replaced `xrange()` with `range()` in test file

### Bug Fix
Fixed a pre-existing bug in `get_domain()` function where the result of `host.split(":", 1)[0]` was not being assigned back to the `host` variable.

### Test Updates
- Updated test expectations for Python 3's `random` module behavior (different shuffle sequences with the same seed)
- Changed expected test result from `/80` to `/24` to match Python 3's random generator

### Configuration
- Updated `tox.ini` to test against Python 3.8, 3.9, 3.10, 3.11, and 3.12 instead of Python 2.6 and 2.7

## Verification

All existing unit tests pass successfully:
- ✅ `test_external_links_per_domain_is_obeyed`
- ✅ `test_max_external_links_is_obeyed`
- ✅ `test_max_internal_links_is_obeyed`

The changes are minimal and surgical, preserving all existing functionality while ensuring compatibility with modern Python versions.

items.title: Fix CI/CD Pipeline: Exclude external wifipumpkin3 code from ruff formatting checks
items.body:
This PR fixes the failing CI/CD pipeline by addressing the root cause: ruff format checks were running on external wifipumpkin3 code that needed reformatting.

## 🎯 Problem

The CI/CD pipeline was completely broken with all test jobs failing across Python versions 3.9-3.12 due to:
- 63 files in `src/wifi/wifipumpkin3/` failing ruff format checks
- External wifipumpkin3 code being included in formatting validation despite being excluded from linting

## 🔧 Solution

Updated the CI workflow to exclude external code from format checks:

```yaml
# Before: Format check runs on all source files
ruff format --check src/

# After: Format check excludes external wifipumpkin3 code
ruff format --check src/ --exclude src/wifi/wifipumpkin3/
```

## 📊 Results

**Before:** All workflows failing with "63 files would be reformatted" error
**After:** Format checks pass with "7 files already formatted"

**Validation:**
- ✅ Ruff formatting: 7 files already formatted (passes)
- ✅ Ruff linting: All checks passed with existing exclusions
- ✅ Application functionality: AnonSuite 2.0.0 CLI working properly
- ✅ Security scan: Already passing independently

## 🎯 Impact

This is a minimal surgical fix that changes only 1 line in the CI workflow to exclude external code from formatting validation while preserving all existing linting rules, security checks, and application functionality. The CI/CD pipeline is now fully operational across all Python versions.

Fixes #5.

make docker-test-all

make docker-test-py312

items.body:
# Update to 2025 Data Dump, Removal of Python 2 Compatibility,  and Introducing UV

  This PR updates the project to work with the September 2025 StackExchange data dump and removes
  legacy Python 2 compatibility code. Additionally, we introduce the use of `uv` to better handle Python dependencies and managing the project.

## Changes

  Archive URL Update:
  - Updated default archive URL from https://ia800107.us.archive.org/27/items/stackexchange to
  https://archive.org/download/stackexchange_20250930/stackexchange_20250930
  - Points to September 2025 data dump (previously Sept 2014)

  Database Auto-Creation:
  - Added ensureDatabaseExists() function to automatically create target database if it doesn't exist
  - Connects to postgres database first to check/create target database
  - Uses safe SQL identifier composition to prevent SQL injection
  - Eliminates manual createdb step for basic usage

  Documentation Updates:
  - Updated README to reference September 2025 data dump
  - Added uv as recommended dependency manager
  - Updated all command examples to use uv run python instead of plain python
  - Clarified that manual database creation is now optional (script will create database if it does not exist)
  - Updated StackExchange

  Python 2 Compatibility Removal:
  - Removed six library dependency from pyproject.toml
  - Replaced six.print_() with standard print() throughout codebase
  - Replaced six.next() with built-in next() in row_processor.py
  - Replaced six.moves.urllib.request.urlretrieve() with urllib.request.urlretrieve() in
  load_into_pg.py
  - Removed raw_input compatibility shim


  ## Testing

  All Python files pass validation:
  - AST parsing validation
  - Compilation checks
  - Module import tests

 ## Compatibility

  Requires Python 3.8+, which matches the existing requires-python = ">=3.8" in pyproject.toml.
  Python 2 reached end-of-life in January 2020.

  ---

This project has not had a contribution is some time, but I am hoping someone out there still wants to maintain this repo. I was able to take the repo and help me easily import various StackExchange topics into my test server for pgFirstAid. Having some test data will help me validate health checks added to pgFirstAid as it grows. Thanks to all that contributed to this project before me!

items.title: Add convert to Python 3, add native MP3 support
items.body:
This commit modernizes AudioCompare with native MP3 support using the miniaudio library, eliminating the need for the external lame binary.

Why: The previous implementation relied on a 32-bit Linux lame binary that doesn't work on modern systems (macOS, Windows, 64-bit Linux). This created portability issues and added subprocess overhead. Native MP3 support provides better cross-platform compatibility and improved error handling.

Core Changes:
- Add miniaudio library for native MP3 decoding (pure Python bindings)
- Refactor InputFile class to detect and handle WAV/MP3 formats
- MP3 files are decoded to memory on initialization
- WAV files continue to be read from disk on demand
- Remove lame binary (354KB) and all subprocess code
- Add descriptive error messages for unsupported formats

Python 3 Migration:
- Migrate all Python 2.7 code to Python 3.8+ compatibility
- Fix print statements, xrange, dict methods, integer division
- Fix NumPy 2.0 compatibility (np.complex_ → np.complex128)
- Add IEEE Float WAV support (format_tag 3)

Dependency Management:
- Add pyproject.toml with uv/hatchling build system
- Add miniaudio>=1.59 dependency
- Define test dependencies as optional extras
- Create audiomatch entry point

Testing Infrastructure:
- Add comprehensive end-to-end test suite (17 tests)
- Download real WAV files from public sources
- Generate MP3 test files using ffmpeg (graceful degradation if unavailable)
- Test WAV/MP3 matching, format compatibility, error handling
- All tests pass: 11 WAV tests + 6 MP3 tests

Test Results:
- 100% backward compatibility: all existing WAV functionality preserved
- Cross-format matching: WAV ↔ MP3 matching works perfectly (score 52.6)
- Error handling: descriptive messages for corrupted/unsupported files

Files Modified:
- InputFile.py: Complete refactor with format detection and MP3 decoding
- pyproject.toml: New dependency management with uv
- README.md: Updated docs with MP3 support and technical details
- test/: New comprehensive test suite with fixtures

Files Removed:
- lame: 32-bit Linux binary no longer needed
- audiomatch: Shell script replaced by Python entry point
- test/*.py: Old test files replaced with pytest-based suite

items.title: Rebuild Observatory Dashboard + Fix ES Aggregate Event Dispatch
items.body:
### Summary

This PR completely rebuilds the Protean Observatory from a single 3,788-line monolithic HTML file into a professional multi-page dashboard with server-rendered templates, and fixes a critical bug where event-sourced aggregate events were never reaching the outbox or Redis.

---

### Observatory Dashboard Rebuild

Replaces the fragile embedded `dashboard.html` with a modular, multi-page architecture inspired by Sidekiq UI and Celery Flower.

**Architecture:**
- **FastAPI + Jinja2 templates** — 6 page templates extending a shared `base.html` layout with DaisyUI drawer navigation
- **Modular route system** — `routes/` package with focused modules: `handlers.py`, `processes.py`, `eventstore.py`, `infrastructure.py`, `pages.py`
- **Vendored static assets** — TailwindCSS 4, DaisyUI 5, D3.js v7 bundled as static files (no CDN, no Node.js build step)
- **Modular JavaScript** — `core.js` (SSE, polling, fetch helpers), `charts.js` (D3 sparklines/area/bar/gauge), plus per-page modules (`overview.js`, `handlers.js`, `processes.js`, `eventstore.js`, `infrastructure.js`, `messages.js`)

**Views:**

| View | Description |
|------|-------------|
| **Overview** | KPI cards with D3 sparklines (throughput, latency, error rate, in-flight, DLQ depth), subscription health table, recent errors, activity timeline, event type breakdown |
| **Handlers** | Registry metadata + subscription status + per-handler trace metrics merged into a unified handler-centric view with search, filtering, sorting, sparklines, and expandable detail panels |
| **Messages** | Message lifecycle explorer with trace-based stage tracking, error filtering, pagination |
| **Processes** | Process Manager monitoring — PM summary cards, sortable table, instance explorer from event store transitions |
| **Event Store** | Aggregate stream metadata, event store stats (instance counts, head positions), outbox status, per-stream detail with instance breakdown |
| **Infrastructure** | Connection health tiles (database, broker, event store, cache), Redis metrics, server info with URI credential masking |

**Cross-cutting polish:**
- Keyboard navigation (chord shortcuts: `g+o`, `g+h`, etc.; `/` to focus search; `r` to refresh; `?` for help modal)
- Deep linking via URL query params for all filter/sort state
- CSV export for all data tables
- Loading state management (progress bar + KPI fade during fetches)
- SSE-driven real-time updates with centralized polling

**Performance:**
- `/api/traces/overview` combines stats + timeline into a single XRANGE scan with 3-second in-memory cache
- Outbox `count_by_status()` optimized from `SELECT * ... len(results)` to `SELECT COUNT(*)` via `.limit(1).all()` using `.total`

---

### Bug Fixes

**Event-sourced aggregate events never reaching outbox/Redis:**
- Root cause: `UnitOfWork.commit()` iterated over `self._sessions` to push events to outbox, but ES aggregates are persisted to the event store without opening a database session — so no outbox INSERT ever happened
- Fix: iterate over `all_events` keys (providers with events) instead of `_sessions`, lazily initializing a database session when one is missing for outbox writes

**`handlers_for()` crash for external events without `part_of`:**
- External events registered via `register_external_event` have no `part_of` aggregate, causing `event.meta_.part_of.meta_.stream_category` to raise `AttributeError`
- Fix: add a fallback scan across all registered stream categories when `part_of` is `None`, matching handlers by event `__type__`

---

### Command Processing Observability

- Added trace emission to synchronous command processing (`CommandProcessor`), making commands visible in the Observatory dashboard with `handler.started`, `handler.completed`, and `handler.failed` traces
- Added `domain.trace_emitter` lazy property for `TraceEmitter` access
- Added `pm.transition` trace events for Process Manager state transitions in the Engine

items.title: feat: add extra_commands support to presets with odoo_version markers
items.body:
We have to handle a particular packaging case for old odoo.
- `vatnumber` is needed for odoo <= 13.0, see [here](https://github.com/odoo/odoo/blob/13.0/requirements.txt#L40)
- `vatnumber` package needs `2to3` to build which has been removed from recent setuptools
- in the past we had pinned "setuptools<58" as a workaround:
   - in [oca ci](https://github.com/OCA/maintainer-quality-tools/pull/695)
   - in [emoi](https://gitlab.trobz.com/packages/emoi/-/blob/py3/emoi/utils/helper.py#L322)
- then on oca ci, the workaround was moved to a dedicated container for legacy odoo: see [here](https://github.com/OCA/oca-ci/pull/98)

This PR is an attempt at handling it properly in odoo-venv: 

## Summary
- Add `extra_commands` field to presets.toml to run additional commands at specific stages during venv creation
- Commands can be conditional based on `odoo_version` markers (e.g., `odoo_version <= '13.0'`)
- Support for environment variables in commands (e.g., `UV_NO_BUILD_ISOLATION=1`)
- Verbose output shows condition and environment for each executed command

## Details
The new `extra_commands` feature allows running arbitrary commands at three stages:
- `after_venv` - after virtual environment creation
- `after_requirements` - after installing all requirements
- `after_odoo_install` - after installing Odoo in editable mode

Each command can have:
- `command`: list of command arguments
- `when`: marker expression (supports `odoo_version` and standard packaging markers)
- `stage`: one of the three stages above
- `env`: optional dict of environment variables

## Concretely for `vatnumber` 
```toml
[[common.extra_commands]]
command = ["uv", "pip", "install", "setuptools<58.0"]
when = "odoo_version <= '13.0'"
stage = "after_requirements"

[[common.extra_commands]]
command = ["uv", "pip", "install", "vatnumber==1.2"]
when = "odoo_version <= '13.0'"
stage = "after_requirements"
env = { UV_NO_BUILD_ISOLATION = "1" }
```

items.title: Fix MCQ multi-select grading (#36)
items.body:
## Summary
- **Backend**: Replaced `next()` with set-based comparison in all 4 MCQ handler methods + submission service. Multi-select MCQs now grade with partial credit: `score = (correct selected) / (total correct)`, `passed_all_tests` only on exact set match. Input format: JSON array `["a","b"]` for multi-select, plain string for single-select (backwards compatible).
- **Frontend**: `McqInput` detects checkbox mode from `inputConfig.type`, toggles a `Set<string>`, emits JSON array. `McqFeedback` shows "Partially Correct" amber banner for 0 < score < 100 and joins multiple option texts.
- **Types/passthrough**: Added `selected_options`, `correct_options`, `score` optional fields to 3 type interfaces and 2 ProblemSet.vue result-building locations.
- **Tests**: 7 new unit tests covering exact match, partial (1 of 2), over-selection, zero-correct, single-select backwards compat, empty array validation, and invalid ID validation. All 103 handler tests pass.

Closes #36

## Test plan

items.title: Upgrade to Django 3.x and Python 3.10+
items.body:
Modernizes django-form-utils to support Django 3.0-3.2 and Python 3.6+. Drops support for Django < 2.2 and Python 2.x.

## Core Changes

**Removed Python 2 compatibility layer:**
- `django.utils.six` → native Python 3 (`six.text_type()` → `str()`, `six.iteritems()` → `.items()`)
- `@python_2_unicode_compatible` decorator removed

**Fixed Django 3.x compatibility:**
- `BoundField` import: `django.forms.forms.BoundField` → `django.forms.boundfield.BoundField`
- Widget `render()` methods now accept `renderer` parameter
- Template tags use dict context instead of `template.Context`

**Rewrote `ClearableFileInput` widget rendering:**
Django 3.x moved from `format_output()` to template-based widget rendering. Overrode `render()` to maintain custom template formatting:

```python
def render(self, name, value, attrs=None, renderer=None):
    # Decompress and render subwidgets individually
    rendered_widgets = []
    for i, widget in enumerate(self.widgets):
        widget_name = '%s_%s' % (name, i)
        rendered_widgets.append(widget.render(widget_name, value[i], ...))
    
    # Apply custom template
    if current_value:
        return mark_safe(self.template % {'input': rendered_widgets[0], 'checkbox': rendered_widgets[1]})
    return mark_safe(rendered_widgets[0])
```

## Configuration Updates

- `setup.py`: Python 3.6-3.10, Django 2.2-3.2
- `tox.ini`: Updated test matrix, removed South dependency
- Test runner: `MIDDLEWARE_CLASSES` → `MIDDLEWARE`, added `TEMPLATES` configuration

## Test Updates

Accounts for Django 3.x behavioral changes:
- HTML output includes `required` attributes
- Field widgets are deep-copied (identity checks replaced with type checks)

All 48 tests passing on Django 3.0, 3.1, 3.2.

items.title: Realtime crypto: Redis-backed streams, Binance trade ingestor, 24x7 bars, runners and demo OMS
items.body:
### Motivation
- Enable cross-process realtime state for crypto (BTCUSDT) so API `/realtime/*` reads the same live bars/signals/ops across processes. 
- Replace full-stream `XRANGE` replays to avoid duplicate processing and permit incremental consumption by multiple consumers. 
- Provide a repeatable paper-only OMS demo that executes against realtime price to validate end-to-end realtime plumbing.

### Description
- Replaced process-local realtime caches with Redis-backed keys (with in-memory fallbacks for tests): `realtime:bars:{symbol}:{tf}` (LIST), `realtime:signals:{symbol}:{tf}` (STRING), `realtime:ops:summary` (STRING), `realtime:hot:{name}` (LIST), matching API key formats. (changes in `services/bar_builder/bar_builder/storage.py` and `services/realtime_signal_engine/realtime_signal_engine/state_store.py`).
- Switched stream consumption to incremental reads with persisted cursors (`cursor:market_events`, `cursor:bar_close:{tf}`) using `XREAD` blocking reads where available and updating cursors after processing; falls back to prior behavior for in-memory stubs. (changes in `services/bar_builder/bar_builder/consumer.py` and `services/realtime_signal_engine/realtime_signal_engine/engine.py`).
- Added a Binance public trade ingestor `services/stream_ingestor/binance_trade_ingestor.py` that connects to Binance WS (`<symbol>@trade` or combined streams), canonicalizes trades to the repo market-event schema and publishes to `stream:market_events` via `XADD` (capped). 
- Added runnable service entrypoints `services.bar_builder.run` and `services.realtime_signal_engine.run`, a demo OMS executor `scripts/demo_crypto_oms_realtime_exec.py` that reads the latest `realtime:bars:BTCUSDT:15m` close and performs draft→approve→execute (paper), and corresponding `Makefile` targets (`redis`, `rt-btc-ingest`, `rt-btc-bars`, `rt-btc-signals`, `rt-btc-demo-order`) plus documentation `docs/CRYPTO_REALTIME_PAPER_DEMO.md` with run steps.
- Made `build_bar_windows` support 24x7 UTC windows for crypto exchanges (`CRYPTO`, `BINANCE`, `BINANCE_PUBLIC`) so 15m/60m bars bucket continuously; added regression test `tests/test_bar_windows_crypto_24x7.py` and updated/added cross-process realtime tests `tests/test_realtime_redis_cross_process_keys.py`. Also minor UX/error extraction improvements in the kiosk action component.

### Testing
- Formatting and linting: ran `python -m black` and `python -m ruff check` on modified files and all checks passed. 
- Automated tests: ran `PYTHONPATH=.:packages/data python -m pytest -q tests/test_bar_windows_crypto_24x7.py tests/test_realtime_redis_cross_process_keys.py tests/test_late_event_correction_policy.py` and the invoked suites passed (`5 passed`).
- Smoke: validated runner/ingestor entrypoints and `Makefile` targets exist and the demo script reads `realtime:bars` keys and posts OMS draft/approve/execute payload shapes (script requires Redis + API to run end-to-end in an environment).

------

items.title: feat: agent() task primitive + AI framework integration examples
items.body:
## Summary

This PR positions Flux as the orchestration layer for AI workloads with two major additions:

### `agent()` — LLM-calling Flux tasks (`flux.tasks.ai`)

A factory that creates Flux `@task` functions for calling LLMs. Each agent is a regular task with full observability, retries, and durability.

```python
from flux.tasks.ai import agent

researcher = agent("You are a researcher.", model="ollama/llama3", name="researcher")
writer = agent("You are a writer.", model="ollama/llama3", name="writer")

@workflow
async def pipeline(ctx: ExecutionContext[dict]):
    research = await researcher(f"Research: {ctx.input['topic']}")
    return await writer(f"Write about: {ctx.input['topic']}", context=research)
```

**Features:**
- **Provider abstraction** — `ollama/`, `openai/`, `anthropic/` model strings
- **Tool use** — pass existing `@task` functions as tools (schema auto-generated from signature + docstring)
- **Structured output** — `response_format=PydanticModel` for typed JSON responses
- **Stateful mode** — conversation history across invocations within a workflow
- **Composable** — works with `parallel()`, `Graph`, `pause()`, all Flux primitives

### AI Framework Integration Examples

Side-by-side examples showing Flux orchestrating LangChain, LangGraph, and CrewAI:

| Framework | Example | Pure Flux Equivalent |
|-----------|---------|---------------------|
| LangChain | Conversational Agent | `conversational_agent_ollama.py` |
| LangChain | RAG Pipeline (Chroma) | `rag_agent_ollama.py` |
| LangGraph | Multi-Agent Code Review | `multi_agent_code_review_ollama.py` |
| CrewAI | Multi-Agent Code Review | `multi_agent_code_review_ollama.py` |
| CrewAI | Blog Post Writer | `blog_post_writer_ollama.py` |

### Infrastructure Changes

- **`task.with_instance_options()`** — instance-level option merging for post-creation customization
- **`task.func`** — property exposing the wrapped function for tool schema introspection
- **`flux/tasks/` package** — split monolithic `tasks.py` into `builtins.py`, `pause.py`, `call.py`, `graph.py` modules
- **Multi-agent code review** — updated from `parallel()` to `Graph` DAG
- **Documentation** — AI Agents section added to task patterns docs

## Test plan
- [x] 545 tests pass (11 new + 534 existing)
- [x] Pre-commit hooks pass on all files
- [x] Manually tested LangChain examples against local Ollama (conversational, RAG, code review)
- [x] Manually tested CrewAI examples against local Ollama (code review, blog writer)
- [x] Manually tested agent() against Ollama (basic call, context pipeline, structured output)
- [x] PR review comments addressed and resolved

items.title: Richer AccessorPath API
items.body:
This pull request introduces enhancements and cleanup to the path handling logic in `pathable/paths.py`, focusing on improving the interface for accessing and manipulating child paths, as well as updating and expanding the related test coverage. The most significant changes include adding floor division operators for path navigation, removing deprecated methods, and updating tests to reflect the new interface.

### Path interface improvements

* Added `__floordiv__` and `__rfloordiv__` methods to allow using the floor division operator (`//`) for accessing child paths, providing a more intuitive and Pythonic way to traverse paths. (`[pathable/paths.pyR162-R213](diffhunk://#diff-97204c858188d235e2027743dbf615b39e3a1fb67cc1bf6168d2704fa068c165R162-R213)`)
* Updated the `__getitem__` and `get` methods to use the new floor division operator internally, ensuring consistent behavior and error handling. (`[pathable/paths.pyR162-R213](diffhunk://#diff-97204c858188d235e2027743dbf615b39e3a1fb67cc1bf6168d2704fa068c165R162-R213)`)
* Added an `exists` method for checking if a path exists, and improved docstrings for better clarity throughout the class. (`[pathable/paths.pyR162-R213](diffhunk://#diff-97204c858188d235e2027743dbf615b39e3a1fb67cc1bf6168d2704fa068c165R162-R213)`)

### Deprecated method cleanup

* Removed several deprecated methods (`getkey`, `iter`, `iteritems`, `content`, `open`) to streamline the API and encourage use of the updated interface. (`[pathable/paths.pyR162-R213](diffhunk://#diff-97204c858188d235e2027743dbf615b39e3a1fb67cc1bf6168d2704fa068c165R162-R213)`)

### Test suite updates

* Added new tests for the floor division operator in `TestLookupPathFloorDiv`, ensuring correct error handling and value retrieval. (`[tests/unit/test_paths.pyR809-R841](diffhunk://#diff-c18e2e75a029ba4cabcc608ca2fe3ef7b95551458089c10c554e10b5c239baacR809-R841)`)
* Updated existing tests to use the new `//` operator instead of the old `get` method for accessing child paths. (`[tests/unit/test_paths.pyL829-R862](diffhunk://#diff-c18e2e75a029ba4cabcc608ca2fe3ef7b95551458089c10c554e10b5c239baacL829-R862)`)

items.title: feat: wire complete LangGraph StateGraph + SSE streaming (#15)
items.body:
## Summary
- Wire the complete LangGraph pipeline graph with all nodes, edges, fan-out/fan-in, and conditional routing
- Replace stub SSE streaming with real `astream_events` from graph execution

## Pipeline Flow
```
input_processor
    ↓ (fan-out × 5)
run_council_agent [parallel]
    ↓ (fan-in)
cross_examination
    ↓ (fan-out × 5)
score_axis [parallel]
    ↓ (fan-in)
strategist_verdict
    ↓
decision_gate
    ↓
┌─────────────┬──────────────────┬──────────────────┐
│ GO          │ CONDITIONAL      │ NO-GO            │
│ doc_gen     │ conditional_rev  │ feedback_gen     │
│ code_gen    │ → doc/feedback   │ → END            │
│ deployer    │                  │                  │
│ → END       │                  │                  │
└─────────────┴──────────────────┴──────────────────┘
```

## Key Technical Decisions
- `PipelineState` extends `VibeDeployState` with custom `merge_dicts` reducers for `council_analysis` and `scoring` fields
- Fan-out via `add_conditional_edges` + `Send` API for true parallel execution
- Fan-in is automatic — LangGraph waits for all `Send` executions to complete
- `MemorySaver` checkpointer enables `interrupt()`/`resume` for CONDITIONAL path

## SSE Events Emitted
| Event | When |
|-------|------|
| `council.phase.start` | Pipeline begins |
| `council.node.start/complete` | Each node starts/finishes |
| `council.agent.analysis` | Per-agent analysis results |
| `council.verdict` | Final score + decision |
| `deploy.complete` | Deployment result with live URL |
| `council.error` | Pipeline errors |
| `council.phase.complete` | Pipeline done |

Closes #15

items.body:
This commit moves roadmap template rendering from the `/erk:objective-create` command prompt into two new exec commands, eliminating ~120 lines of inline markdown templates and reducing dual-construction errors. ## Files Changed
Files Changed ### Added (2 files) - `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/objective_render_roadmap.py` - New exec command to render complete roadmap markdown from structured JSON input - `tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_objective_render_roadmap.py` - Unit tests for roadmap rendering with validation and output verification ### Modified (7 files) - `.claude/commands/erk/objective-create.md` - Replace inline Steelthread/Linear/Single roadmap templates with instructions to construct JSON and call `objective-render-roadmap`; reduce metadata documentation by ~60 lines - `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/group.py` - Register new `objective-render-roadmap` command - `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/objective_save_to_issue.py` - Add `--validate` flag to run objective validation after creation and include results in output - `.claude/skills/erk-exec/reference.md` - Document new `objective-render-roadmap` command and `--validate` flag - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/metadata/roadmap.py` - Fix `group_steps_by_phase()` to handle non-dotted step IDs (e.g., "1", "2") and multi-dot IDs - `packages/erk-shared/tests/unit/github/metadata/test_roadmap_frontmatter.py` - Add tests for undotted, mixed, multi-dot, and letter-based step ID grouping - `tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_objective_save_to_issue.py` - Add tests for `--validate` flag in JSON and display formats
## Key Changes - **New `erk exec objective-render-roadmap` command**: Reads structured JSON from stdin (phases, steps, descriptions) and outputs a complete `## Roadmap` section with phase headers, markdown tables, test sections, and auto-generated metadata block — eliminates ~80 lines of template from prompt - **Roadmap metadata consistency by construction**: Shared `RoadmapStep` and `render_roadmap_block_inner()` logic ensures tables and metadata block stay in sync; removes manual YAML formatting from prompt - **Add `--validate` flag to `objective-save-to-issue`**: Runs objective validation immediately after issue creation and includes validation results in JSON/display output, eliminating separate command and "fix and re-run" loop - **Fix `group_steps_by_phase()` step ID parsing**: Now correctly handles undotted IDs ("1", "2"), multi-dot IDs ("1.2.3"), and letter IDs ("A"), grouping them into appropriate phases instead of skipping malformed entries - **Simplified objective creation prompt**: Replace 3 full templates with minimal JSON examples and unified instructions; reduce metadata block documentation since it's now auto-generated ## Implementation Plan
Implementation Plan (Issue #7179) # Plan: Push objective-create template logic to erk exec commands ## Context The `/erk:objective-create` command file (`.claude/commands/erk/objective-create.md`) contains ~120 lines of inline markdown templates (Steelthread, Linear, Single) that instruct Claude to manually construct both human-readable roadmap tables AND machine-readable YAML metadata blocks. This is the #1 source of objective creation errors because: 1. **Dual construction** - Claude must write the same step info in two places (table rows AND YAML entries) and keep them in sync 2. **Template bloat** - 3 templates x ~30 lines each consumes prompt budget 3. **Format fragility** - Claude can get table column count wrong, YAML indentation wrong, or metadata block markers wrong The infrastructure to render these correctly already exists in `erk_shared` (`render_roadmap_block_inner()`, `wrap_roadmap_tables_with_markers()`, `RoadmapStep`/`RoadmapPhase` dataclasses). It just isn't exposed as exec commands. ## Opportunities Identified ### Opportunity 1: `erk exec objective-render-roadmap` (HIGH impact) **What it does:** Takes structured JSON describing phases/steps, outputs complete roadmap markdown section with tables + metadata block, guaranteed consistent. **Input** (stdin JSON): ```json { "phases": [ { "name": "Steelthread", "description": "Minimal vertical slice proving the concept works end-to-end.", "pr_count": "1 PR", "steps": [ {"id": "1.1", "description": "Minimal infrastructure"}, {"id": "1.2", "description": "Wire into one command/path"} ], "test": "End-to-end acceptance test for steelthread" }, { "name": "Complete Feature", "description": "Fill out remaining functionality.", "pr_count": "1 PR", "steps": [ {"id": "2.1", "description": "Extend to remaining commands"}, {"id": "2.2", "description": "Full test coverage"} ], "test": "Full acceptance criteria" } ] } ``` **Output:** Complete `## Roadmap` section including: - Phase headers (`### Phase 1: Steelthread (1 PR)`) - Phase descriptions - 5-column markdown tables (Step | Description | Status | Plan | PR) - Test sections per phase - Metadata block (auto-generated from steps, guaranteed in sync) **Why:** Eliminates ~80 lines of template from the prompt. Guarantees table-metadata consistency by construction. Uses existing `RoadmapStep`, `render_roadmap_block_inner()`. **Prompt change:** Replace all 3 templates' roadmap sections with: ``` Construct a JSON object describing the phases and steps, then run: echo '' | erk exec objective-render-roadmap Include the output in your objective proposal. ``` ### Opportunity 2: `--validate` flag on `objective-save-to-issue` (MEDIUM impact) **What it does:** After creating the issue, automatically runs `objective check` and includes validation results in the JSON output. **Current Step 6** (3 commands): ```bash # 1. Write plan file (Claude) # 2. Create issue erk exec objective-save-to-issue --session-id= --format=display # 3. Validate erk objective check --json-output ``` **Proposed** (1 command): ```bash erk exec objective-save-to-issue --session-id= --format=display --validate ``` **Output addition:** ```json { "success": true, "issue_number": 42, "issue_url": "...", "validation": { "passed": true, "errors": [] } } ``` **Why:** Eliminates a sequential command and the "fix and re-run" loop instruction from the prompt. The exec command can run validation internally and report in one shot. ## Implementation Plan ### Phase 1: `objective-render-roadmap` exec command **Files to create:** - `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/objective_render_roadmap.py` - The exec script - `tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_objective_render_roadmap.py` - Unit tests **Implementation:** 1. Click command that reads JSON from stdin 2. Validates input structure (phases, steps, ids) 3. For each phase: renders markdown header, description, table, test section 4. Collects all steps into flat list of `RoadmapStep` objects 5. Calls `render_roadmap_block_inner()` to generate metadata block 6. Outputs complete `## Roadmap` section **Key reuse:** - `RoadmapStep` from `erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.roadmap` - `render_roadmap_block_inner()` from same module - Click context injection pattern from `AGENTS.md` ### Phase 2: Add `--validate` to `objective-save-to-issue` **Files to modify:** - `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/objective_save_to_issue.py` - Add `--validate` flag - `tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_objective_save_to_issue.py` - Add test for validation **Implementation:** 1. Add `--validate` click option (default False) 2. After successful issue creation, if `--validate`: invoke objective check logic 3. Include validation results in output JSON 4. Non-zero exit only if *creation* fails, not validation (validation errors are informational) ### Phase 3: Update `objective-create.md` prompt **File to modify:** - `.claude/commands/erk/objective-create.md` **Changes:** 1. Replace inline templates with instructions to construct JSON and call `objective-render-roadmap` 2. Keep non-roadmap sections (Goal, Design Decisions, Implementation Context, etc.) as prompt guidance - these are creative content, not structural 3. Simplify Step 6 to use `--validate` flag 4. Remove the separate `erk objective check` step 5. Significantly reduce the metadata block documentation (it's now auto-generated) ## Verification 1. Create a test objective using `/erk:objective-create` and verify: - Roadmap tables render correctly with proper columns - Metadata block is present and valid - `erk objective check` passes 2. Run unit tests for the new exec command 3. Run existing objective tests to ensure no regressions
--- Closes #7179 To checkout this PR in a fresh worktree and environment locally, run: ``` source "$(erk pr checkout 7181 --script)" && erk pr sync --dangerous ```

- Add SessionStart hook script (`session-start-briefing.sh`) for automatic context injection at session start

Bumps [requests](https://github.com/psf/requests) from 2.32.4 to 2.33.0.
Release notes

Sourced from requests's releases.

v2.33.0

2.33.0 (2026-03-25)

Announcements

  • 📣 Requests is adding inline types. If you have a typed code base that uses Requests, please take a look at #7271. Give it a try, and report any gaps or feedback you may have in the issue. 📣

Security

  • CVE-2026-25645 requests.utils.extract_zipped_paths now extracts contents to a non-deterministic location to prevent malicious file replacement. This does not affect default usage of Requests, only applications calling the utility function directly.

Improvements

  • Migrated to a PEP 517 build system using setuptools. (#7012)

Bugfixes

  • Fixed an issue where an empty netrc entry could cause malformed authentication to be applied to Requests on Python 3.11+. (#7205)

Deprecations

  • Dropped support for Python 3.9 following its end of support. (#7196)

Documentation

  • Various typo fixes and doc improvements.

New Contributors

The Requests team has decided to revert this feature as long term maintenance of it is proving to be unsustainable in its current iteration.

Deprecations

items.title: Enrich get_transaction_info with ERC-4337 user operations
items.body:
### Motivation
- Reduce unnecessary follow-up calls by agents by surfacing ERC-4337 (Account Abstraction) User Operations alongside transaction data when present.
- Improve UX for AI consumers by adding a lightweight summary of user operations and conditional guidance only when relevant.
- Keep the transaction tool robust: secondary failures when querying user-ops must not break the primary transaction response.

### Description
- Added a new `UserOpsLite` model and `user_operations` field to `TransactionInfoData` in `blockscout_mcp_server/models.py` to represent AA operations.
- Implemented `_transform_user_ops` in `blockscout_mcp_server/tools/transaction/_shared.py` to convert raw AA operations responses into the compact summary format.
- Updated `get_transaction_info` to fetch transaction details and account-abstraction operations concurrently via `asyncio.gather`, softly handle errors from the ops endpoint, attach `user_operations` to the response, add a conditional instruction when user ops exist, and add a pagination note when `next_page_params` is present (`blockscout_mcp_server/tools/transaction/get_transaction_info.py`).
- Updated docs and descriptors in `API.md`, `SPEC.md`, and `gpt/action_tool_descriptions.md` and expanded unit and integration tests under `tests/tools/transaction/test_get_transaction_info.py` and `tests/integration/transaction/test_get_transaction_info_real.py`.

### Testing
- Ran formatting/linting: `ruff check . --fix` and `ruff format .` and then `ruff check .` / `ruff format --check .`, all checks passed.
- Ran the full unit test suite with `pytest`, resulting in `377 passed, 73 deselected` (all selected unit tests passed).
- Ran the integration tests for transaction info with `pytest -m integration -v tests/integration/transaction/test_get_transaction_info_real.py`, resulting in `3 passed, 1 skipped` (skipped case due to unavailable API data in that scenario).

Closes #280

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_b_697360846f9083238f40fa8b4b239cc8)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Transaction info now includes a summary of associated ERC-4337 User Operations when present.
  * User operations are fetched concurrently with other transaction details for faster enrichment.

* **Documentation**
  * Updated endpoint descriptions and instructions to explain how to view and paginate User Operations.

* **Tests**
  * Added integration and unit tests covering presence, absence, errors, and pagination of User Operations.

* **Chores**
  * Project version bumped to 0.13.0.dev6.

<sub>✏️ Tip: You can customize this high-level summary in your review settings.</sub>

items.title: feat: graceful timeout via scratchpad review documents
items.body:
## Summary
- Reviewers write to `/tmp/{perspective}-review.md` throughout investigation via WriteFile (enabled by `--quiet` → `--yolo` mode)
- On timeout (exit 124), the scratchpad persists and `parse-review.py` extracts the verdict from `## Verdict:` markdown headers, producing a partial review (confidence 0.3) instead of a useless FAIL fallback
- Fallback chain: scratchpad with JSON → stdout with JSON → scratchpad without JSON → stdout (existing fallback)
- Default timeout bumped 300s → 600s

## Changes
| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `scripts/parse-review.py` | `extract_verdict_from_markdown()`, `is_scratchpad()`, `extract_notes_summary()`, modified `write_fallback()` and `fail()` |
| `scripts/run-reviewer.sh` | Export `PERSPECTIVE`, `{{PERSPECTIVE}}` template replacement, scratchpad fallback chain, timeout=600 |
| `action.yml` | Timeout 300→600, parse step reads scratchpad, review document artifact upload |
| `templates/review-prompt.md` | `{{PERSPECTIVE}}` placeholder, Review Workflow section |
| `agents/*-prompt.md` (×5) | One-liner WriteFile instruction in Output Format |
| `defaults/config.yml` | Timeout 300→600 |
| `tests/test_parse_review.py` | 5 new scratchpad tests (54 total) |
| `tests/fixtures/` | 2 new fixtures (complete + partial scratchpad) |

## Test plan
- [x] `python3 -m pytest tests/ -v` — 54/54 passing
- [x] `shellcheck scripts/run-reviewer.sh` — clean
- [x] `python3 -m py_compile scripts/parse-review.py` — clean
- [ ] E2E on moonbridge: push, trigger review, verify scratchpad artifact uploaded
- [ ] Forced timeout test: set `timeout: 60` for one reviewer, verify partial review produces WARN (not FAIL)
- [ ] Verify WriteFile works in CI `--quiet` mode

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

## Release Notes

* **New Features**
  * Per-perspective review documents now generated and available as separate artifacts alongside verdicts for enhanced visibility.
  * Increased model processing timeout from 300 to 600 seconds for improved reliability.

* **Documentation**
  * Enhanced review workflow templates with structured investigation notes, findings, and verdict tracking across all review perspectives.

Bumps [future](https://github.com/PythonCharmers/python-future) from 0.18.2 to 1.0.0.
Release notes

Sourced from future's releases.

v1.0.0

The new version number of 1.0.0 indicates that the python-future project, like Python 2, is now done.

The most important change in this release is adding support for Python 3.12 (ba1cc50 and a6222d2 and bcced95).

This release also includes these fixes:

  • Small updates to the docs
  • Add SECURITY.md describing security policy (0598d1b)
  • Fix pasteurize: NameError: name 'unicode' is not defined (de68c10)
  • Move CI to GitHub Actions (8cd11e8)
  • Add setuptools to requirements for building docs (0c347ff)
  • Fix typos in docs (350e87a)
  • Make the fix_unpacking fixer more robust (de68c10)
  • Small improvements to shell scripts according to shellcheck (6153844)

v0.18.3

This is a minor bug-fix release containing a number of fixes:

  • Backport fix for bpo-38804 (c91d70b)

  • Fix bug in fix_raise.py fixer (3401099)
  • Fix newint bool in py3 (fe645ba)

items.title: fix: split issue intake workflows into separate files
items.body:
## Problem

The combined `agents-issue-intake.yml` workflow was causing issues because it tried to handle two disparate elements that kept interfering with each other:

1. **agent_bridge**: Turn an Issue (from GitHub issues tab) into a bootstrap PR
2. **chatgpt_sync**: Turn text input into a set of issues in bulk

## Solution

Split them into separate workflows:

| Workflow | Purpose | Triggers |
|----------|---------|----------|
| `agents-63-issue-intake.yml` | Issue-to-PR bootstrap | Issue events + manual |
| `agents-chatgpt-sync.yml` (new) | Bulk issue creation from topic files | Manual only |

Both now call the Workflows repo reusable workflows directly with the appropriate mode.

## Files Changed

- **`agents-63-issue-intake.yml`**: Unchanged (already working)
- **`agents-chatgpt-sync.yml`**: New dedicated workflow for ChatGPT sync
- **`agents-issue-intake.yml`**: Removed (was the problematic combined workflow)

## Testing

After merge, the ChatGPT Sync workflow can be tested via:
1. Go to Actions tab
2. Select "ChatGPT Sync" workflow
3. Click "Run workflow"
4. Provide either `source` (path in repo) or `raw_input` (small text) or `source_url`
